items.title: New line of hope in discovering the Nannup Four

children.content: The vice-president of a search team that solved two high-profile missing persons cases in Tasmania turns his attention to the Nannup Four case in Western Australia after it was featured in the Expanse podcast.

items.title: After 70 bags of fruit were stolen in one weekend, farmer closes roadside stall

image.altText: Several bags of mandarins on a black table with a sign reading "$5 per bag" placed inside of a corrugated steel shelter.

children.content: A West Australian mandarin grower is shutting his seasonal roadside stall in the South West after 17 years due to people stealing up to 40 per cent of the stock laid out each day.

items.title: Pauline Hanson reveals Gina Rinehart's role in One Nation policy making

children.content: Pauline Hanson speaks of her "respect" for billionaire Gina Rinehart, becoming emotional during a speech in Perth recalling her time in detention.

articles.title: Billionaire backer Rinehart dictates to Hanson, Chalmers says

children.content: Treasurer Jim Chalmers hits out at Pauline Hanson's ties to Gina Rinehart and questions whether she actually stands for battling Australians.

image.altText: Treasurer Jim Chalmers in a navy suit and maroon tie, pressing his lips tightly.

articles.title: Hundreds protest against Pauline Hanson as WA Labor seats on 'hit list'

children.content: A crowd of about 400 protesters demonstrates against One Nation's Pauline Hanson as she speaks at a sundowner event in Perth's eastern suburbs.

image.altText: A group of protesters holding anti-Pauline Hanson signs.

gallery.altText: Pauline Hanson speaks to reporters among a crowd, wearing a brown and black jacket.
Four people stand inside a shed with three holding big novelty cheques.

items.title: BHP bracing for strike after WA workers back industrial action

image.altText: A water tank with the words 'Welcome to Port Hedland' and the BHP logo

children.content: Unionised workers at BHPs operations in Port Hedland could walk off the job as soon as next week after endorsing protected industrial action.

items.title: Beachside towns in WA face difficult decisions as erosion gets worse

children.content: A week after Western Australia experienced one of its worst storms in decades, some stretches of coast are crumbling into the ocean at an alarming rate. Millions of dollars' worth of infrastructure is at risk heading into winter.

gallery.altText: Sand bags sit in front a cliff of sand. A building sits a few metres behind.
A drone shot shows the ocean and erosion near a road. Behind it sits a recreation centre, playground, bike track and houses.
Glen Trebilcock lifts a shovel full of sand, in front of a fork lift holding an open sand bag

items.title: NDIS inquiry told 240,000 people to be 'exited' from scheme

children.content: A Senate inquiry into the government's National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) changes has been told 240,000 people will be "exited" from the scheme. Follow live.

image.altText: A man in a navy suit and tie looks sternly into camera.

items.title: Amex ordered to compensate man whose ex accessed his data

children.content: Australia's privacy commissioner has threatened a complainant with legal action to prevent the full disclosure of the findings of a long-running investigation into American Express's information security, which found widespread technology failures.

image.altText: A modern office building with an American Express sign.

items.title: New species found in Australia's most 'pristine' marine parks

children.content: Researchers have catalogued dozens of new marine species found in the depths of Australia's most remote waters. The result is a window into a rarely glimpsed world.

gallery.altText: A close-up image of an oval-shaped, soft-bodied organism with 10 legs.
A yellow sea star sits on black background next to a white scientific label.

thumbnail.altText: A bright orange furry crab.
A close-up image of an oval-shaped, soft-bodied organism with 10 legs.
A yellow sea star sits on black background next to a white scientific label.

items.title: More couples opt for micro-weddings and elopements amid cost-of-living crisis

children.content: Melbourne couple Averill and Lloyd Johnson pulled off their dream wedding for just $3,000, compared to the average wedding cost of about $38,000.

image.altText: Groom wearing a suit and top hat and bridge wearing a black dress, holding up their middle fingers with rings on them.

items.title: How you and your superannuation came to be aboard SpaceX

children.content: If your superannuation is managed by any of Australia's big superannuation funds, chances are you're about to become a highly valued financial backer to Elon Musk.

image.altText: smoke and flames emerge from a spacex launch in texas

items.title: US forecasters say potentially record-breaking El Niño underway

children.content: There is a high chance this year's Pacific climate phenomenon will be among the strongest on record, which could lead to hotter temperatures across the globe.

image.altText: Storm clouds drop heavy rain in the distance on a western Queensland property

children.content: Don't let your confidence be shaken or stirred by this week's test of how much you've been paying attention.

items.title: Town abandoned by gas company faces tough choices as transition hits home

children.content: For many, getting off gas and electrifying their homes is a personal choice. But what happens when an entire town is abandoned by a gas company?

items.title: Study reveals complex links between social media use and teen wellbeing

children.content: A decade-long study following almost 1,200 Melbourne school students finds a "small increased risk" of depressive symptoms for teenagers using social media at least two hours a day.

image.altText: teens using their phones with various social media related illustrations

children.content: The ASX opens strongly on the back of Wall Street rally and oil prices tumble. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: Lauren can play like a 'normal kid' thanks to 600,000 strangers

children.content: Lauren Zeller has a one-in-a-million bleeding disorder, but strangers' blood donations give her a chance to play like other kids.

items.title: Major PFAS class action could lead to multi-billion dollar claim figure

children.content: Hundreds of businesses and organisations are part of a major Victorian Supreme Court class action against chemical manufacturer 3M.

items.title: International students face a double financial blow

children.content: When Jolie Cao chose to do her undergraduate university degree in Australia, she knew it would be expensive. But an additional financial pressure has made things "really hard".

image.altText: A woman with black hair in a puffer jacket smiling to camera.

items.title: 'I don't see why you would choose this': Fears for swimming's future

children.content: Kyle Chalmers spent more than $5,000 to compete at the Australian Swimming Trials, and he says that sort of thing could drive stars away from the sport.

image.altText: Kyle Chalmers at the Australian Swimming Trials.

items.title: Massive whale graveyard found in deep ocean off Australia

children.content: Hundreds of whale fossils, including those from extinct species new to science, range in age from recent to 5 million years old and are also home to undescribed deep-sea creatures.

items.title: How One Nation could disrupt Australia's progressive stronghold

children.content: The idea that One Nation could come close to the government benches was absurd six months ago, but it is a prospect Victorian Labor MPs and insiders are increasingly discussing.

items.title: Person dies after contracting rare mosquito-borne disease in WA

items.title: Why Bunnings moving a store 10km down the road has caused such a stir

children.content: A plan by hardware giant Bunnings move its Bunbury store 10km to a neighbouring shire has angered and concerned residents and businesses.
